title: Lysander town board moves forward on data center moratorium as massive project looms
summary: Lysander town board is advancing a proposed six-month moratorium on data center development, with a public hearing scheduled for May 7, 2026. This regulatory action suggests local government is evaluating infrastructure and land-use impacts from large-scale data center projects in the region.

political_signal.description: Town board initiating regulatory pause on data center development, suggesting local concerns about infrastructure capacity, environmental impact, or uncoordinated regional growth. May indicate constituent pressure or need for comprehensive land-use planning.

processing_notes: Article references 'massive project looms' but specific project details not provided in excerpt
This is a neighboring jurisdiction (Lysander) action but relevant to Syracuse CIO for regional infrastructure planning and potential competitive/collaborative implications
